incorrects.description: Student selected 60: added pencils and markers instead of finding the difference

steps.text: You added pencils and markers. The question asks how many more pencils than markers. Find the difference between the two bars.

incorrects.description: Student selected 40: read only the pencils value instead of comparing

steps.text: That's how many pencils sold total. We need to compare pencils to markers. How much taller is the pencils bar than the markers bar?

incorrects.description: Student selected 15: likely compared wrong categories or misread scale

steps.text: Let's think about this together. Look at the two highlighted bars. Pencils reach 40, markers reach 20. Subtract to find the difference.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System highlights the difference between Pencils bar (40) and Markers bar (20), showing the 20-unit gap

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Pencils sold 40. Markers sold 20. To find how many more, subtract: 40 minus 20 equals 20. The answer is 20.
